name: Fuenfría and Navacerrada Passes Loop
name_original: Parque Nacional de Guadarrama – circular por Puerto de Fuenfría, Puerto Navacerrada y Silla del Rey
description: This difficult 26.2-mile mountain biking route in Parque Nacional de Guadarrama offers a challenging and scenic ride through the mountains.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. The region features extensive Valsaín pine forests, providing a network of wide forest tracks and technical singletracks. Mountainous landscapes include constant ascents and descents, natural paths, and trails along rivers like the Eresma and Valsaín. This area is part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, ensuring preserved natural environments for exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso The most popular mountain bike trail is Valle de Valsaín y Cueva del Monje — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores the Valsaín valley and features the notable Cueva del Monje. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cerro Puerco Trenches, Monk's Cave, and King's Chair Loop, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.4 km) path. This trail combines historical sites with varied terrain and scenic viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuenfría and Navacerrada Passes Loop, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through the high mountain passes of the Sierra de Guadarrama, often completed in about 4 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso is defined by pine forests, mountainous terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest tracks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Real Sitio De San Ildefonso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Real Sitio de San Ildefonso?
A: The region offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from wide forest tracks within the extensive Valsaín pine forest to challenging technical singletracks. You'll encounter varied mountainous terrain with constant ascents and descents, natural paths, and scenic routes along rivers like the Eresma and Valsaín, all set within the beautiful Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Real Sitio de San Ildefonso?
A: Yes, while many routes feature challenging ascents, there are options for less experienced riders. Out of the 36 available routes, 6 are rated as easy and 13 as moderate, providing gentler terrain and shorter distances suitable for families or those new to mountain biking. Look for routes that stick to wider forest tracks rather than technical singletracks.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The trails often lead through the stunning Valsaín pine forest and offer views of prominent peaks like Peñalara. You can also explore routes along the Valsaín River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350398423/royal-sites-and-pesquerias-reales-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Sites and Pesquerías Reales Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the historic Royal Fisheries. Other routes pass by unique formations like the Cueva del Monje, featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350387262/valle-de-valsain-y-cueva-del-monje-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Valsaín y Cueva del Monje — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; tour.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. You can find several waterfalls, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca del Asno Gorge and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2494973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro Grande Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For lakes, highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Birds&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque spots to visit.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Real Sitio de San Ildefonso?
A: The area is generally excellent for mountain biking, but an early start is recommended on summer weekends, especially in popular areas like the Valsaín pine forest, to avoid crowds. The Sierra de Guadarrama can experience snow in winter, particularly at higher elevations like the Fuenfría and Navacerrada Passes, which might make some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its historical grandeur. You can visit the magnificent 18th-century Royal Palace of La Granja de San Ildefonso with its famous gardens and fountains, or explore the Royal Glass Factory. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080351279/cerro-puerco-trenches-monk-s-cave-and-king-s-chair-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Puerco Trenches, Monk's Cave, and King's Chair Loop&lt;/a&gt;, even incorporate historical sites directly into the trail experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Real Sitio de San Ildefonso?
A: The mountain bike routes in Real Sitio de San Ildefonso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Many riders praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Valsaín pine forests, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents and descents found within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike loops available?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961275597/fuenfria-and-navacerrada-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenfría and Navacerrada Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding 42.1 km ride through high mountain passes. Another difficult option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350405069/la-atalaya-y-rio-piron-desde-la-granja-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Atalaya y río Pirón desde La Granja — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 43 km and requires good fitness and advanced skills.

Q: Is it possible to reach the trailheads using public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead are not provided, Real Sitio de San Ildefonso is generally accessible. For routes starting in Valsaín or La Granja, local bus services connect to Segovia, which has broader transport links. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to specific starting points like Boca del Asno or Valsaín trails.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: As the region is part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, there may be specific regulations regarding trail use, especially in protected zones. While general mountain biking is permitted on designated trails, it's always recommended to check the official National Park website for any specific permit requirements or restrictions that might apply to certain areas or times of the year.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Real Sitio de San Ildefonso?
A: Many trailheads, especially those near popular starting points like Valsaín, La Granja, or Boca del Asno, typically offer designated parking areas. Given the popularity of the Valsaín pine forest, arriving early, particularly on weekends, is recommended to secure a parking spot.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike routes in the area?
A: While the guide doesn't explicitly list dog-friendly routes, many natural trails within the Valsaín pine forest and less technical forest tracks are generally suitable for dogs, provided they are kept under control. Always check local signage and National Park regulations regarding pets, especially in protected wildlife areas, to ensure compliance.

The curious name of this place is due to the chair carved in the stone that alludes to the King's throne. It is surrounded by a beautiful grove that provides good shade on hot days, an ideal place to take a break and follow the various alternative routes that are in the area.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as difficult and is best suited for experienced mountain bikers. It demands a high level of physical endurance due to significant elevation changes and advanced technical skills to navigate challenging sections. Be prepared to dismount and push your bike on some stretches.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, typical of the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll encounter granite massifs, extensive pine forests, and high mountain scrubland. Specific areas, especially near Puerto de Navacerrada, feature challenging 'rock garden zones' and 'super chunky' sections, alongside smoother forest tracks. You might also ride on historical paths, including remnants of a Roman road, and encounter water ruts.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty and historical significance. You'll pass by notable spots such as Fuente de la Reina, Fuenfría Pass, and Navacerrada Pass. The King's Chair (Silla del Rey) is also a key feature. Expect breathtaking vistas from ridges and summits, views of prominent peaks like Peñalara, and ancient forests.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Nacional de Guadarrama, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always follow park regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this mountain biking route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is most favorable. During winter, conditions can be very challenging with snow and ice, especially at higher elevations like Puerto Navacerrada, requiring specialized equipment and experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the National Park?
faqs.answer: There is no entrance fee to access the Parque Nacional de Guadarrama. However, as it is a protected area, specific regulations apply to activities like mountain biking. Always stick to authorized trails to avoid fines and protect the environment. No specific permits are generally required for mountain biking on designated routes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is available at key access points like Puerto de Fuenfría and Puerto de Navacerrada. These areas often have designated parking lots. During peak seasons or weekends, parking can fill up quickly, so arriving early is advisable.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Cordel de Santillana, the Madrid Way of Saint James, and the Ruta Verde Puerto de Navacerrada - Cercedilla. It also briefly touches the Camino Schmidt and the Sendero de los Reales Sitios.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Parque Nacional de Guadarrama is rich in biodiversity. While mountain biking, you might spot various bird species, including raptors. Mammals like deer, wild boar, and even Iberian ibex inhabit the park, though they are often shy. Always maintain a respectful distance and do not disturb the wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Puerto de Fuenfría and Puerto de Navacerrada are popular areas with some facilities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and even accommodation options in the nearby towns or directly at the passes, especially at Puerto de Navacerrada, which is a small ski resort area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult nature and length of the route, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather. A helmet is essential, and consider knee/elbow pads. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the komoot app is highly recommended for navigation.
